title: T-011 — POST /api/scanner/audit batch ingest (06-a)
prompt:
# T-011 — scanner audit batch ingest

PRD: 06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.1 FR-SC-BE-012, §5.3, §16.1 telemetry
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Accept up to 50 audit entries per call, write them async to scanner_audit_log, deduplicate via client_operation_id, and emit telemetry events.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/audit/route.ts` (POST) accepting `{ entries: AuditEntry[] (1..50) }`.
2. Insert via single Drizzle insert..on conflict (client_operation_id) do nothing.
3. Emit one PostHog event per entry per §16.1 mapping (scanner.scan.success/error, scanner.fefo_deviation, etc.).
4. Auth required; org_id/user_id stamped server-side; ignore any client-provided org_id.

## Files
- apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/audit/route.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/src/server/services/scanner-audit.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/__tests__/api/scanner-audit.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a batch of 3 entries with unique client_operation_id values, when POST /api/scanner/audit runs, then 3 rows are inserted into scanner_audit_log and the response is { inserted: 3, skipped: 0 }
2. Given a batch where two entries share the same client_operation_id with an existing row, when POST runs, then the response is { inserted: 1, skipped: 2 } and the existing row is unchanged
3. Given a batch of 51 entries, when POST runs, then it returns 400 SC_AUDIT_BATCH_TOO_LARGE

## Test strategy
- RED: write scanner-audit.test.ts asserting insert + dedup + 51-entry rejection before implementation
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner test api/scanner-audit'

## Risk red lines
- Do not let client override org_id or user_id
- Do not block the request on PostHog failure (fire-and-forget)
- Do not exceed batch size 50

pipeline_inputs.description: Batch (up to 50) audit ingest endpoint with client_operation_id dedup and PostHog event fan-out per §16.1.
pipeline_inputs.details: POST handler validates the batch (max 50 entries), stamps org_id/user_id server-side, performs a single insert..on conflict (client_operation_id) do nothing, and dispatches PostHog events per §16.1 mapping. Failures in PostHog must not fail the request.
out_of_scope: Do not implement retention cron here
Do not implement client-side queueing here
Do not implement P2 sync-queue endpoint here
acceptance_criteria: Given 3 entries with unique client_operation_id values, when POST /api/scanner/audit runs, then 3 rows are inserted and response is { inserted: 3, skipped: 0 }
Given two entries with a duplicate client_operation_id, when POST runs, then response is { inserted: 1, skipped: 2 } and the existing row is unchanged
Given a batch of 51 entries, when POST runs, then it returns 400 SC_AUDIT_BATCH_TOO_LARGE
test_strategy: RED: write scanner-audit.test.ts asserting insert + dedup + 51-entry rejection before implementation
risk_red_lines: Do not let client override org_id or user_id
Do not block the request on PostHog failure
